notes: Secure Digital memory card format; primary focus of study. Won the format war to become dominant removable storage standard
Competing memory card format; larger physical size. Continued in professional photography but largely superseded by CFexpress
Sony proprietary memory card format; respondents using Sony products were most brand-aware. Discontinued by Sony in favor of SD
Competing memory card format from Olympus and Fujifilm; discontinued around 2010 in favor of SD
Dominant method of transferring photos from digital cameras to PCs at time of study
External device for reading memory cards via USB; emerging alternative to direct camera-to-PC cable transfer
Primary driver of memory card awareness and adoption; owned by vast majority of study participants
Personal Digital Assistants; used by some respondents primarily for business. Superseded by smartphones
Portable digital music player; only a few respondents owned one. Superseded by smartphones
Primary medium for long-term storage of photos and music; respondents burned CDs to free hard drive space
Analog video storage; dominant method for storing camcorder video at time of study
Respondents identified wireless as desired future solution; limited to PDA beaming at study time. Now ubiquitous via WiFi/Bluetooth/AirDrop
Few respondents had home LANs for data transfer; now standard in most households
Owned primarily by families with children; largely superseded by smartphone video
Older memory card format referenced by respondent discussing card reader compatibility issues
